data.title: Coinbase CEO: Quantum computing is a problem that can be solved and will not disrupt the blockchain.
data.description: In an interview at the World Liberty Forum, Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong stated that quantum computing will not destroy blockchain, making it a very solvable problem. He pointed out that Coinbase established a quantum advisory committee last month and maintains regular contact with major blockchain networks to discuss pathways to a post-quantum cryptographic world. Currently, the Ethereum Foundation has listed post-quantum security as a top strategic priority, and the Solana Foundation has begun testing quantum-resistant digital signatures on its testnet. Furthermore, Brian Armstrong also expressed his stance on issues such as the CLARITY bill draft and the regulatory power over prediction markets in the US market structure negotiations.

data.title: SBI Holdings to Acquire Majority Stake in Singapore's Coinhako
data.description: SBI Holdings has announced its intention to acquire a majority stake in Coinhako, a cryptocurrency exchange based in Singapore. According to NS3.AI, the acquisition will be executed through SBI Ventures Asset, a subsidiary of SBI Holdings. Coinhako is currently operating under a Major Payment Institution license granted by the Monetary Authority of Singapore.

data.title: Macron Prepares for Global Stage Clash with Trump Over Social Media
data.description: French President Emmanuel Macron is gearing up for a significant international engagement, potentially setting the stage for a confrontation with U.S. President Donald Trump regarding social media and free speech. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the anticipated clash as Macron seeks to assert his stance on these critical issues. The upcoming event is expected to draw global attention as both leaders have previously expressed differing views on the regulation and role of social media platforms in society. Macron's approach may challenge Trump's policies, emphasizing the broader debate on balancing free speech with the responsibilities of digital platforms.

data.title: Planalto Concerns Over Evangelical Reaction to Carnival Tribute to President Lula
data.description: The Brazilian government is reportedly concerned about potential backlash from evangelical groups following a Carnival parade honoring President Lula. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the Planalto's apprehension regarding the event's reception among religious communities. The parade, which celebrated the president, has sparked discussions about its impact on Lula's relationship with evangelical voters. The government is closely monitoring the situation to gauge public sentiment and address any arising issues.

data.title: Amazon Surpasses Walmart as World's Largest Company by Revenue
data.description: Amazon has overtaken Walmart to become the largest company globally by revenue. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this significant shift in the retail landscape. This development marks a pivotal moment in the competition between the two retail giants, with Amazon's expansive e-commerce platform and diverse business operations contributing to its rise. The change underscores the growing influence of digital commerce and the shifting dynamics in global retail markets.

data.title: IMF Urges China to Rebalance Its Economy
data.description:
The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has reiterated its call for China to adjust its economic structure. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the IMF's concerns about China's reliance on investment-driven growth and the need for a shift towards consumption-led expansion. The IMF emphasized that such a transition is crucial for sustainable economic development.

China's current economic model, heavily dependent on investments, has been a subject of international scrutiny. The IMF suggests that a rebalancing towards consumption would not only stabilize China's economy but also contribute positively to global economic dynamics.

The call for economic restructuring comes amid broader discussions on China's role in the global economy and its impact on international markets. The IMF's recommendations are part of ongoing efforts to encourage economic policies that promote long-term stability and growth.

China's response to these recommendations remains a focal point for economists and policymakers worldwide, as the country navigates its economic future amidst changing global conditions.

data.title: Central Banks Shift from U.S. Treasuries to Gold Amid Geopolitical Tensions
data.description: Central banks worldwide are increasingly moving away from U.S. Treasuries in favor of gold, reflecting a significant shift in global financial strategies. According to Jin10, this trend is driven by heightened geopolitical tensions and concerns over the stability of traditional financial assets. Prominent investor Ray Dalio is reportedly focusing on these changes, suggesting a potential realignment in global markets. As geopolitical events continue to unfold, the impact on financial markets could be substantial, prompting investors to reassess their portfolios.

data.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg Defends Company Actions in Youth Harm Trial
data.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg defended his company's actions during a trial focused on the impact of social media apps on youth. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Zuckerberg's stance that Meta prioritizes user safety and well-being. The trial examines whether social media platforms contribute to mental health issues among young users. Zuckerberg emphasized Meta's commitment to implementing measures that protect users, including age restrictions and content moderation. The case has drawn significant attention as it addresses broader concerns about the influence of social media on youth. Zuckerberg's testimony is part of ongoing efforts to assess the role of technology companies in safeguarding young users.

data.title: SEC Chairman: Regulators should not panic over cryptocurrency price declines
data.description: U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission Chairman Paul Atkins stated that regulators should not panic over the decline in cryptocurrency prices. Atkins added that regulators' role is not to worry about daily market fluctuations, but rather to focus on information disclosure and clarity in long-term regulation.

data.title: Analysis: SOPR has fallen to the 0.92–0.94 range, indicating marginal improvement in the macro environment, but a structural bull market for BTC has not yet been established.
data.description: Bitfinex released an analysis report pointing out that declining inflation and rising expectations of interest rate cuts in the US market provide psychological support for risk assets, but the crypto market is more likely to exhibit phased fluctuations rather than a one-sided trend. The expansion of the Federal Reserve's balance sheet reduces systemic liquidity risk, which historically benefits scarce assets such as Bitcoin. However, the current pace of liquidity recovery is slow, and spot Bitcoin selling pressure re-emerged at the beginning of this week, with cumulative sell-offs reaching billions of dollars. Although the market's ability to absorb selling pressure has improved compared to before, on-chain indicators show that the adjusted SOPR (Spent Output Profit Ratio) has fallen to the 0.92–0.94 range, reflecting that most cryptocurrencies are shifting to a loss-making state, and structural pressure remains. The current macroeconomic environment provides some liquidity buffer for the crypto market, but it is not enough to support a sustained bull market. Bitcoin has short-term tactical rebound potential, but a long-term structural upward trend still requires clearer signals of declining inflation and sustained spot demand.

data.title: Argentina's Economic Growth Contrasts with Decline in Formal Employment
data.description: Argentina is experiencing economic growth, yet formal employment is on the decline. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that labor reform has become a crucial test for Milei's market-oriented plan and his ability to implement structural changes. The situation underscores the challenges facing the country's economic policies as they aim to balance growth with sustainable employment.

data.title: Economic Indicators Released: Philly Fed, Jobless Claims, and Pending Home Sales
data.description:
Several key economic indicators were released this morning, providing insights into the current state of the U.S. economy. Bespoke Investment Group posted on X that the Philadelphia Federal Reserve's manufacturing index, jobless claims, and pending home sales figures were among the data points shared. The Philadelphia Fed's index, a measure of manufacturing activity in the region, showed mixed results, indicating fluctuations in economic conditions.

Jobless claims, a critical indicator of labor market health, revealed a slight increase, suggesting potential challenges in employment stability. Meanwhile, pending home sales data highlighted trends in the housing market, reflecting changes in consumer behavior and economic confidence.

These indicators are closely watched by economists and policymakers as they provide valuable insights into economic trends and potential future developments. The data released today will likely influence discussions on monetary policy and economic strategy moving forward.

data.title: Europe Urged to Strengthen Financial Ties for Future Autonomy
data.description: European Central Bank Executive Board member Frank Elderson emphasized the importance of Europe enhancing its financial connections to maintain control over its future. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Elderson's call for Europe to 'get our act together' in order to safeguard its autonomy. Elderson's remarks underscore the need for strategic financial collaboration within Europe to navigate global economic challenges effectively. His comments reflect ongoing discussions about the continent's economic resilience and the necessity for unified financial strategies to ensure long-term stability and independence.

data.title: Ineos Faces Credit Downgrade Amid Delayed Recovery
data.description: Chemicals giant Ineos has experienced a credit score downgrade by S&P Global Ratings. Bloomberg posted on X that the agency cited a prolonged recovery timeline for the company, owned by UK billionaire Jim Ratcliffe, indicating that a sustained recovery is not expected before 2028.

data.title: Rwanda's Central Bank Raises Key Interest Rate to 7.25%
data.description: Rwanda's central bank has increased its key interest rate from 6.75% to 7.25%, marking a significant monetary policy adjustment. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the central bank's decision as part of efforts to address inflationary pressures and stabilize the economy. The monetary policy committee's move aims to curb rising inflation and ensure economic stability amid global financial uncertainties. This adjustment reflects the central bank's proactive stance in managing the country's economic challenges and maintaining financial stability. The decision comes as Rwanda navigates complex economic dynamics, including external factors impacting its financial landscape. The central bank's action underscores its commitment to safeguarding economic growth and controlling inflation.

data.title: Raspberry Pi Shares Reach New High Amid Market Activity
data.description: Raspberry Pi shares surged to a peak of 550.5 pence on Wednesday, marking a significant milestone for the company. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impressive performance of Raspberry Pi in the stock market. The increase in share value reflects growing investor confidence and interest in the company's offerings. This development comes amid broader market movements and economic factors influencing stock prices. Raspberry Pi's latest achievement underscores its position in the technology sector, attracting attention from both investors and analysts. The rise in share price is seen as a positive indicator of the company's potential for future growth and expansion.

data.title: BlackRock deposited 1,270 BTC and 15,409 ETH into Coinbase, totaling $115 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, BlackRock deposited 1,270 BTC (worth $84.92 million) and 15,409 ETH (worth $30.22 million) into Coinbase, and may continue to deposit more assets.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Considers North American Trade Deal Without Canada
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on February 19, The New York Times reported that U.S. President Donald Trump is contemplating a North American trade agreement that excludes Canada.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Trump's Proposal for Japanese-Backed Gas Plant in Ohio Raises Concerns
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has proposed a plan for Japan to construct a large gas power plant in Ohio, aiming to significantly boost electricity supply in the region. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that while the initiative promises to meet growing energy demands, it also presents potential challenges for other stakeholders in the energy sector. The project is expected to address electricity shortages, but it may also impact competitors and alter the dynamics of the local energy market. The plan underscores the ongoing efforts to enhance energy infrastructure, though it raises questions about the implications for existing energy providers.

data.title: Traditional Finance Giants Embrace Blockchain for Asset Tokenization
data.description: Real World Assets (RWA) tokenization is transitioning from unsuccessful initial projects to a burgeoning multi-trillion dollar market with the potential to transform the financial landscape. According to NS3.AI, prominent traditional financial institutions, including BlackRock, Franklin Templeton, and JP Morgan, are integrating blockchain technology to facilitate quicker and more efficient asset trading and settlement processes. While this development presents significant opportunities, it also brings challenges such as regulatory risks and concerns over oracle reliability. This evolution suggests a future where traditional finance and blockchain technology converge under institutional oversight.
